mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity cash flows, directly reducing present value of future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment above 4.5% indicates weakening labor demand that compresses corporate revenue forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of observations when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer and investment demand, which forces equity valuations lower over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals immediate supply-side bottlenecks that trigger short-term equity sell-offs within the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not precede negative 5-day returns in post-shock recovery periods, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above 3.5% forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening that discounts future earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 3.5% correlates with positive forward returns or shows no statistical relationship in walk-forward tests over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly scales corporate profit expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter when GDP expands.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.02, or returns are negative during expansion quarters in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls alongside a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports consumer spending and equity valuations, generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE does not yield positive 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term loses significance in regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index crossing above 115.0 indicates capacity utilization approaching peak levels that drive short-term equity momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO above 115.0 fails to predict positive 5-day returns or shows negative correlation in out-of-sample periods, or Rank IC drops below 0.01.
